entry.title: Q4 2025 Quarterly Release Highlights: SAP BTP Security and Identity & Access Management
entry.content:
In the last quarter of 2025, we release a number of new features, as well as the SAP Key Management Service.

Want the full overview for SAP Cloud Identity Services? You’ll find a list of all new feature announcements for SAP Cloud Identity Services in the SAP Cloud Identity Services Release Notes on the SAP Help Portal.

SAP Cloud Identity Services: Use Data Control Language (DCL) to Define Authorization Policies

Developers define authorization policies in SAP Cloud Identity Services, using an SQL-like language - the data control language (DCL). Administrators can restrict base policies and combine authorization policies into a new authorization policy. For more details, please check the SAP Help Portal.

SAP Key Management Service

We released the SAP Key Management Service (KMS), which puts customers in control of their data across SAP cloud services and products. By managing their own encryption keys, customers decide exactly who can access their information.

With SAP KMS, data remains inaccessible to any external party, including SAP, government agencies, or legal authorities, unless the customer explicitly authorizes access. The service enables customers to securely create, manage, and control the encryption keys used to protect their data, and helps ensure that encryption and decryption can occur only with their approval.

SAP Cryptographic Library

The latest SAP Cryptographic Library release (version 8.6) supports quantum-safe cryptography and contains updated compliance certifications. It introduces a quantum-safe TLS 1.3 handshake using a hybrid key exchange that protects encrypted communications even against future quantum attacks.

In addition, SAP’s FIPS crypto kernel has achieved FIPS 140-3 certification, meeting strict security requirements for regulated industries. Together, these enhancements help customers future-proof their data protection while maintaining compliance. For more information, check our latest blog  as well as release notes 3685428 - Fixes and features in CommonCryptoLib 8.6.2 and 1848999 - Central Note for CommonCryptoLib 8 (SAPCRYPTOLIB).

Application Vulnerability Report for SAP BTP

Frequent security issues in open-source components endanger business data in applications. Use the application vulnerability report to detect and remediate any vulnerabilities in your SAP BTP landscape. The application vulnerability report focuses on detecting publicly-known security vulnerabilities based on Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVEs). It's crucial to solve such vulnerabilities quickly as attackers are generally aware of them and might try to break into vulnerable systems. Check our blog for details.

Stay connected

Want to stay up to date on our services? Join our SAP BTP Security and SAP Cloud Identity Services communities!

entry.title: Partner learning accelerator: Certified academy for data analyst - SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Certification journeys can feel overwhelming.  Content everywhere, limited time, real project pressure.

That’s exactly why we created the Partner learning accelerator: Certified academy for data analyst – SAP Analytics Cloud.

This 6-hour live academy is designed for partners who want to prepare for the SAP Certified – Data Analyst – SAP Analytics Cloud certification with confidence.  Not just theory, but reinforced core concepts, hands-on practice, and exam-relevant topics you’ll actually use in customer projects.

We focus on data modeling, analysis, visualization, and planning—plus how these capabilities come together in real project scenarios.  The thing is, certification readiness isn’t just about passing an exam.  It’s about feeling ready when a customer asks the hard questions.

If you already understand analytics basics and have some exposure to SAP Analytics Cloud, this academy will help you connect the dots and sharpen your skills.

👉 Register now and invest six focused hours in your growth as an SAP partner.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2025/apr/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00009814.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPAnalyticsCloud #SAPLearning #DataAnalytics

entry.title: Re: How to get tax to determine based off tax material cl...
entry.content:
Good day @AbbieSutcliffe 

Thank you for your question.

The simple answer for 0S01 Sales Rebate is, in standard settlement pricing, if the settlement item has no material context, you cannot make TAXM1 dynamically read from Product Master for RETX. TAXM1 is typically taken in this flow as the default or main value which is commonly 1 for condition access, and that explains the behaviour you see.

So, for your setup, this means the following:

  • RETX uses reference condition type TTX1 and access logic that includes ALAND, TAXK1, TAXM1.
  • If your settlement pricing does not have a real material available at item level, TAXM1 will remain faulty because TAXM1 not derived from product master. See KBA 3130828 for some insights.

For public cloud, you can practically:

  1. Maintain tax condition records for the default TAXM1 value used in settlement for example 1.
  2. Then use an access condition table that does not depend on TAXM1. Note that this is dependent on your scope and location.
  3. Ensure you maintain your customer tax classification and departure country, since those are still the key requirements to drive the solution. This is addressed in KBA 3308072 and KBA 3130828.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Business Partner creation $batch fails at “Call Web Service” in SAP Build Process Automation (BPA)
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am using SAP Build Process Automation (BPA) together with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition to execute SAP’s standard template “Mass Create of Business Partners (58Z)”.
My goal is to create Business Partners via the API_BUSINESS_PARTNER OData service using a $batch POST request.

However, the automation fails at the Call Web Service step with the following error:

irpa_core.error.RequestError: Response code 401 (Unauthorized)

The earlier steps run successfully; the issue occurs only when BPA executes the actual $batch POST for Business Partner creation.

Environment

  • Target system: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Main automation tool: SAP Build Process Automation (BPA)
  • Scenario: Create Business Partners via API_BUSINESS_PARTNER $batch
  • Template:  Mass Maintenance of Business Partners (58Z)

 


Current problem

  • BPA reaches the Call Web Service activity that performs:
    POST https://<tenant>-api.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/$batch
  • At this moment, BPA throws:
    401 Unauthorized (irpa_core.error.RequestError)

    Additional data I can provide (sanitized)

    • Communication User / System / Arrangement screenshots
    • BPA logs for HEAD and Call Web Service (request/response headers)
    • If needed, I can test in Postman using the same Communication User and share:
      • GET A_BusinessPartner?$top=1
      • HEAD x-csrf-token: fetch
      • POST $batch

    This may be a basic question, but any guidance or clarification would be greatly appreciated.


This may be a basic question, but any guidance or clarification would be greatly appreciated. 

—Moon14

entry.title: SAP EWM How to Create RF Performance Measurement
entry.content:
SAP EWM How to Create RF Performance Measurement

Introduction

Radio Frequency (RF) performance measurement is crucial for optimizing warehouse operations in SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM). While RF logging capabilities track user activities and system interactions, performance measurement specifically focuses on analyzing response times, throughput, and operational efficiency of RF transactions. This comprehensive guide addresses the implementation of RF performance measurement infrastructure as referenced in SAP, helping warehouse managers identify bottlenecks and optimize their RF-enabled processes.

What is RF Performance Measurement?

RF Performance Measurement in SAP EWM is a specialized monitoring framework designed to capture, analyze, and report on the performance metrics of radio frequency transactions and operations. Unlike standard RF logging which tracks user activities, performance measurement focuses on:

  • Response Time Analysis: Measuring backend processing times for RF transactions
  • End-to-End Performance Tracking: Complete user experience measurement from RF device interaction to system response
  • Throughput Optimization: Analyzing task completion rates and identifying process bottlenecks
  • Resource Utilization Monitoring: Tracking how efficiently RF resources are being utilized in warehouse operations

The framework provides warehouse managers with actionable insights to improve operational efficiency, reduce task completion times, and enhance overall warehouse productivity.

Activate the RF performance tool:
Execute transaction /SCWM/RSRC and activate the flag "RF performance measurement"

  1. Execute the RF transaction:
    Log in to RFUI and process a Warehouse Order (WO).
  2. Run the performance analysis:
    Execute transaction ST13 and enter PERF_TOOL and execute

Enter EWM_RF_ANALYSIS and execute.

On the next screen, press button "Details"

  1. Review the list of executed Function Modules and their runtime.

This tool highlights only the slow-performing Function Modules, allowing you to identify performance bottlenecks in RF processing and focus analysis on specific backend logic.

Be aware: The tool is designed to identify slow RF processing steps and does not trace every activity.

Prerequisites

Before implementing RF performance measures, ensure the following requirements are met:

System Requirements:

  • SAP EWM system with RF Framework activated
  • SAP S/4HANA 2020 or later versions for enhanced performance measurement capabilities
  • ITS Mobile service properly configured for RF device communication

Technical Prerequisites:

  • Performance Framework Module 2 (PFM2) infrastructure activated in the EWM system
  • ST-A/PI (Service Tools for Applications) version 01P or higher installed
  • Sufficient system resources allocated for performance data collection and storage

Authorization Requirements:

  • Administrative access to EWM configuration (SPRO)
  • Authorization for performance measurement transactions
  • Access to Warehouse Monitor (/SCWM/MON) for performance analysis

Step-by-Step Configuration Guide

Step 1: Access Performance Measurement Settings

Navigate to the performance measurement configuration through:

  • SPRO Path: Extended Warehouse Management → Settings → Resource Management → Performance Measurement
  • Transaction Code: Use /SCWM/RFLOGACT for RF-related performance settings
  • Alternative Access: Through Warehouse Monitor (/SCWM/MON) → Resource Management → Performance Analysis

Step 2: Define Performance Indicators

Configure key performance indicators (KPIs) for RF operations:

Standard Performance Metrics:

  • RF transaction response times
  • Task confirmation duration
  • Screen navigation efficiency
  • Validation processing times
  • Exception handling performance

Custom Performance Indicators:

  • Warehouse-specific metrics based on operational requirements
  • Resource group performance comparisons
  • Queue processing efficiency measurements

Step 3: Set Up Measurement Parameters

Configure measurement parameters through the performance framework:

Backend Runtime Measurement:

  • Activate function module execution time tracking
  • Configure threshold values for performance alerts
  • Set up data retention policies for performance records

Frontend Data Collection:

  • Implement optional Badi WORKLOAD_STATISTIC for enhanced metrics
  • Configure upload interface for frontend performance data
  • Define measurement overhead parameters to minimize system impact

Step 4: Configure RF Device Integration

Ensure proper integration between RF devices and performance measurement:

Device Configuration:

  • Verify RF device connectivity through ITS Mobile
  • Configure presentation profiles for performance-optimized screens
  • Set up resource assignments for performance tracking

Communication Settings:

  • Optimize HTTP/HTTPS communication parameters
  • Configure timeout values for performance measurement
  • Implement real-time data synchronization for accurate metrics

Step 5: Activate Performance Monitoring

Complete the activation process:

System Activation:

  • Execute performance measurement activation programs
  • Validate configuration through test transactions
  • Verify data collection is functioning correctly

Testing and Validation:

  • Perform end-to-end testing with actual RF devices
  • Validate performance data accuracy
  • Confirm reporting functionality is operational

Common Configuration Scenarios

High-Volume Warehouse Operations:

  • Configure parallel processing for performance data collection
  • Implement queue-specific performance monitoring
  • Set up automated performance threshold alerts

Multi-Resource Environment:

  • Define resource group-specific performance metrics
  • Configure comparative performance analysis between different resource types
  • Implement workload balancing based on performance data

Monitoring and Reporting

Available Analysis Tools:

  • Warehouse Monitor Integration: Performance metrics accessible through /SCWM/MON
  • Real-time Dashboards: Live performance monitoring capabilities
  • Historical Analysis: Trend analysis and performance pattern identification

Standard Reports:

  • RF transaction performance summaries
  • Resource utilization efficiency reports
  • Except for handling performance analysis
  • Comparative performance metrics across different time periods

Troubleshooting Common Issues

Performance Data Collection Issues:

  • Verify PFM2 infrastructure is properly activated
  • Check system resources allocation for performance measurement
  • Validate Badi implementations if custom performance metrics are used

RF Device Performance Problems:

  • Review of ITS Mobile configuration for optimal performance
  • Analyze network connectivity and response times
  • Optimize RF screen configurations for better performance

Data Analysis Challenges:

  • Ensure proper authorization for performance analysis transactions
  • Verify data retention settings are appropriate for analysis needs
  • Check system performance impact of measurement overhead

Best Practices

Configuration Recommendations:

  • Start with standard performance indicators before implementing custom metrics
  • Configure appropriate data retention periods to balance analysis needs with system performance
  • Implement gradual rollout across warehouse areas to validate configuration

Operational Guidelines:

  • Regular review of performance thresholds and alerts
  • Periodic analysis of performance trends to identify optimization opportunities
  • Integration with existing warehouse management processes and KPIs

Maintenance Considerations:

  • Schedule regular cleanup of performance data using standard cleanup programs
  • Monitor system impact of performance measurement and adjust parameters as needed
  • Keep performance measurement configuration aligned with warehouse operational changes

Conclusion

Implementing RF performance measurement in SAP EWM provides warehouse managers with powerful tools to optimize operations, identify bottlenecks, and improve overall efficiency. The framework enables data-driven decision making by providing detailed insights into RF transaction performance, resource utilization, and operational patterns. By following this comprehensive configuration guide, organizations can establish a robust performance measurement infrastructure that supports continuous improvement in warehouse operations.

The key benefits include enhanced operational visibility, proactive performance management, and the ability to optimize resource allocation based on actual performance data rather than assumptions.

Additional Resources

Related SAP Notes:

  • SAP Note 1595305: Measuring runtimes for RF devices in SAP EWM
  • SAP Note 1690850: Performance measurement in RFUI
  • SAP Note 69455: Service tools for Applications ST-A/PI
  • 3712387 - How to Create RF Performance Measurement in SAP EWM - SAP for Me

Hope that can help you.

Thank you and regards

Rogerio Lima.

entry.title: Re: #CoE#QUSS How to check change logs for custom busines...
entry.content:
Hi David,

Thank you very much for your question. I'm adding the reply from the very similar question:

"Do you mean this app? 

From: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6aa39f1ac05441e5a23f484f31e477e7/76384d8e68e646d6ae5ce897...

to this app?:https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/business-configuration-change-logs?ve...

Display Change Logs

For a selected business configuration, the action Display Change Logs is available in the header section if:

  • at least one database table of the RAP BO of the Business Configuration Maintenance Object Table Logging is active.

  • the Business Configuration Change Logs app is configured as a valid navigation target for the user.

With this action, you can navigate to the Business Configuration Change Logs app.

Could you explain why it is not available in your system? do you have the proper roles, etc?

To access the app, you need to have the following business catalog assigned to your user: SAP_CORE_BC_BCT_LOG_PC - Business Configuration - Change Logs.

This business catalog is available in the business role template: SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT.

To display the change logs for tables, users must have the authorization for the object S_TABU_NAM with Display Change Documents for ACTVT and the name of the table for TABLE

If you could provide some more details, someone should be able to help

Thank you

Jerry"

Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Re: Storage bin determination for synchronous goods receipts
entry.content:
Hello,

I also wanted to know the system logic (I my case for the goods receipt process for production orders)

This SAP Knowledge Base Article explains it. 3249204 - How to determine storage bin when posting goods receipt for production orders - SAP for Me

With Delivery - Goods Movement Bin Determination

  1. Customizing "Assign Storage Bins to Warehouse Process Type", the source storage bin can be assigned to the relevant Warehouse Process type(WPT). 
  2. APP Manage Procuction Versions(C223), the destination bin can be assigned to the finished and semifinished products.
  3. You can use APP Determine Staging Area - Inbound(/SCWM/STADET_IN) to propose the bin.

Synchronous Goods Movement Without Delivery - Storage Bin Determination

  1. APP Manage Procuction Versions(C223), the destination bin can be assigned to the finished and semifinished products.

 Caution for Storage bin determination for synchronous goods receipts: 3709519 - Staging area determination does not consider warehouse process type for sync Goods Movement - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud - SAP for Me: (WPT is not considered as one of the determining factors)

That is what I found on this topic.

entry.title: BPC10 - How to load blank values into master data attribute
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I'm working on BPC10.5 SP21 on NW7.5 SP33. SAP Business Planning and Consolidation, version for SAP NetWeaver 

I have a basic master data load from a flat file (it's a ";" separated file), and for a specific attribute of an existing dimension member the file carries a blank value. However the system doesn't update that attibute from the previous value to the blank value that should be loaded from the file. 

This was the initial mapping in transformation file:

ATTR1 = *COL(12)

Then I tried with:

ATTR1 = *if (*COL(12) = *str() then *str(); *COL(12))

The "if" is managed correctly (I've tried assigning specific fixed strings after the "then" clause), but assigning *str() as a result doesn't overwrite the attribute value.

I've also tried assigning directly *str() to the attribute, but still it doesn't overwrite the attribute value.

I've tried different keywords like "blank" or "none" but with no results. 

Any ideas?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: The Re-emergence of Community in the SAP Ecosystem
entry.content:
I wasn’t able to attend Berlin last year, unfortunately, but I’ve had the same reflections from our local events. To me, there are two major shifts.

First, SAP is clearly opening up and becoming much more open to discussion. The difference between my first TechEd in 2006 and the most recent one I attended is striking—especially the move to open floors where you could actually meet and talk with developers and product owners.

Second, there’s the massive transformation we’re in the middle of, both technically and in terms of mindset. With BTP and the shift to cloud—private and public—we really need each other to understand and navigate what’s happening.

entry.title: Re: Need to Keep Leading Zeros in SAP when using Data Mig...
entry.content:
Good day @liseLily 

Thank you for your question.

You seem to be facing a formatting or display issue and not a true data model loss. BPEXT is an external BP identifier field, so leading zeros can be stored. However, depending on the app, in many UIs, leading zeros are suppressed or not displayed. See KBA 2491623 and 2914703 for more insights.

The most common root cause that the system suppresses numbers during migration is possibly because:

  • Excel converts values like 000000314 to numeric 314 before upload.
  • Then Migration Cockpit receives already-truncated content.

Here is what i suggest:

  • Prepare your migration file with text-safe formatting. For CSV, enforce quotes “000000314” for BPEXT, in Excel template: set BPEXT column to Text before pasting the value. Then reopen your saved file and verify zeros are still present.
  • Load one BP with BPEXT = 000000314 and verify in BP API or table-based report, not only one UI field display.
  • If your UI still shows 314, then check if the stored value is still 000000314 and only display is compressed. If integration to CRM requires exact string, validate via API payload retrieval.
  • Make sure you keep your CRM key in an additional custom field dedicated for exact external ID matching and use that field in CPI mapping. This helps you avoid ambiguity from UI formatting or search normalization.

Step-by-step guide in your tenant you may consider:

In the Project migration app, open you BP migration object and import 1-row test file.

Add an object for BP, open the object. Download template and prepare 1-row file (remember the format mentioned above).

Read BP and confirm BPEXT payload value. If it is different, then you know the document that displays the formatting is the issue here.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: LOSC Partial pick
entry.content:
Dear @LK_WF,

Point 1. LOSC is in scope in Public Edition

The Public Edition 2602 Feature Scope/Innovation document explicitly lists: You can use layout-oriented storage control to use intermediate bins

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/doc/7c9e0bbbd1664c2581b2038a1c7ae4b3/2602.500/en-US/FSD_CE2602.pdf

Point 2. Why LOSC often doesn’t trigger in Public Cloud

SAP Note 3509874 states LOSC won’t trigger only one WT created mainly when:

  • Stock isn’t packed ->LOSC is HU-required
  • A confirmation flag is set -> LOSC is ignored

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3509874

So if you’re trying to enforce full HU/bin move first but your process creates product WTs for partial quantities and/or stock isn’t consistently in HUs, LOSC won’t behave like the dual-stage flow

Point 3. The released Public Cloud API for WTs/WOs exists and which comm scenario to use

SAP Note 3400080 confirms the Warehouse Order and Task (A2X) API service group API_WAREHOUSE_ORDER_TASK_2 for S/4 Cloud and states you must use communication scenario SAP_COM_0353 and mentions the required business catalog for OAuth2

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3400080

Point 4. Public Cloud events

SAP Note 3584400 confirms:

  • Warehouse/Task/Created is not triggered if the task is not created directly in Open status
  • Use business event TaskActivated for the Waiting -> Open transition

SAP Note below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003584400

What is available in Public Cloud is a released toolbox to build a side-by-side workaround:

  • Events including TaskActivated

SAP Note below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003584400

  • A2X APIs for warehouse tasks/orders via SAP_COM_0353 / API_WAREHOUSE_ORDER_TASK_2

SAP Note, API and SAP Help below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003400080

https://api.sap.com/api/WAREHOUSEORDER_0001/overview

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c46da892efa14cea8347e9b872199d6f/3f4ed3d800bb42ce9588fc7fa4051a9a.html

  • And the warehousing API documentation also references operations like splitting warehouse tasks from an existing warehouse order so your split/change tasks idea can be aligned with released content

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c46da892efa14cea8347e9b872199d6f/b05c37a10150450fbff230adcfd8213e.html

Summary:

1. Try to fix LOSC first for your picking flow:

    • Ensure reserve stock is always HU-packed full pallet/HU so LOSC can trigger
    • Ensure the LOSC-related confirmation flag isn’t set in the relevant configuration where it would suppress LOSC
    • Then LOSC can create the interim HU move via intermediate bin your desired two-stage concept

2. If you still need automation now:

    • Use Enterprise Events but specifically include TaskActivated for Waiting -> Open scenarios
    • Use API_WAREHOUSE_ORDER_TASK_2 scenario SAP_COM_0353 to perform released actions like create/cancel/reassign/split depending on your design

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Enrich your process data with reusable business logic with Calculated Attributes
entry.content:
Process analysts are great at finding answers. 

But too often, they’re blocked by one thing: the data doesn’t quite have the dimensions they need. 

With Calculated Attributes, that changes. 

Instead of going back to IT or rebuilding data pipelines, analysts can now add new business logic directly inside their analysis — creating new, reusable attributes on top of existing process data. 

Create once. Use everywhere. 
In the past, defining something like this usually meant writing SIGNAL again and again in different widgets, or worse, keeping versions of the logic in Excel sheets, notes, or someone’s personal workspace. Over time, things drifted, definitions changed, and nobody was quite sure which version was the right one anymore. What seemed like a simple logic update might require hundreds of minor changes in many different places. 

Calculated Attributes put an end to that. 

You can now define your business logic once inside of a Calculated Attribute and it automatically becomes an extension of your process data model. From that moment on, everyone can use it in dashboards, filters, other calculations, and visualizations without rewriting or copying anything. Even colleagues who don’t write SIGNAL are now empowered to make use of these attributes and build their own widgets with drag and drop, benefiting from the work that experts have already done. 
 
The result is a single, shared understanding of your business logic that scales across teams and use cases. Less duplication, less confusion, and a lot more confidence in the numbers you’re looking at. 

 Calculated Attribute that categorizes a case into one of three on-time delivery buckets

 

That’s how your process data stops being static and starts becoming a living model that grows with every new question you ask.  

Check out the help space for more information!

Want to know more? Feel free to reach out!

entry.title: UI5 MessageStrip Error: Assertion failed: Method 'class' must be called with exactly one class name
entry.content:
Hi,

I had an exception in the Console when loading a ui5 page (not Fiori elements), that contains a MessageStrip:

Error: Assertion failed: Method 'class' must be called with exactly one class name

Component: sap.m.MessageStrip
Version affected: 1.143.0+
Version working: 1.142.6
Error: Assertion failed: Method 'class' must be called with exactly one class name
Reproduction: <MessageStrip class="sapUiSmallMargin" text="Test"/>

There are clearly not 2 classes assigned to the MessageStrip. It even occurs if the class tag is not even there.

entry.title: Re: JMS Mastery Series Part 2 - The JMS Revolution
entry.content:
Nice blog @MateuszPiotrowski !

When talking about idempotency, I always like to remind the audience that it does not last forever. The IDs saved are gone after 90 days so the same message could be processed 3 months later. That should be enough for most of the cases but not 100% bulletproof.

BR
Javier

entry.title: What Business Context – Custom Field and Logic Development in Project Budget Report at Item Level
entry.content:
We are developing a customized Project Budget Report at the item level. To proceed, we require the business context for maintaining the relevant field at the item level. Could you please provide the required details at the earliest? 

Thanks.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: What Business Context – Custom Field and Logic Develo...
entry.content:
The business context required for creating Custom Fields for the application Project Budget Report is outlined in the documentation below as Project Header:

App Extensibility: Project Budget Report | SAP Help Portal

As a key user, you can extend the Line Items page of the Project Budget Report app according to your business needs.

Custom Fields

With the Custom Fields app, you can create and maintain custom fields that you can use to enhance applications.

You can create custom fields using the business context Project Header, enable usage of these fields for the Project Budget Report app (data source Extensibility Model for Project Budget Report Line Items) in the UIs and Reports section of custom field maintenance, and publish your custom fields. For more information, see Creating Custom Fields.

I hope this information helps.

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: BAPI_SHIPMENT_CHANGE clean core alternative?
entry.content:
Hi @sh4il3sh 

As per note 3578329 - Frameworks, Technologies and Development Patterns in Context of Clean Core Extensibility BAPIs are upgrade stable and clean core level B.

BAPI_SHIPMENT_CHANGE maintains LE-TRA shipments which are obsolete anyways. I don't think you need to replace the BAPI with anything else. It is good enough if you plan to stick with LE-TRA.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Unlock the Future: Predicting Customer Behavior with SAP Customer Data Platform AI Workbench
entry.content:
Imagine having the insight to predict your customers’ next move. Whether it's anticipating a potential churn or gauging their engagement levels, understanding customer behavior is invaluable. We invite you to our exclusive live session, "Predicting Customer Behaviour with SAP Customer Data Platform AI Workbench," designed to empower you with the tools to foresee your customers' actions.

Session Overview

Join us on March  18, 2026, from 15:00 to 16:30 GMT+2 as we delve into the profound capabilities of the SAP Customer Data Platform AI Workbench. Discover how to create analytic dashboards that reflect potential future customer behaviours by establishing Churn and Customer Lifetime Value models:

  • Forecast Customer Dynamics: Learn to anticipate changes using predictive indicators derived from diverse model runs.
  • Profile and Segment: Utilize insights to categorize customer data into segments, allowing you to respond proactively to predicted behaviors.
  • Harness Pre-trained AI Models: Our session will demonstrate how powerful AI models can analyze customer data in depth to forecast future actions, offering you the ultimate advantage in customer relationship management.

Who Should Attend?

This session is invaluable for anyone keen on transforming customer data into actionable insights, including:

  • Business Practitioners aiming to optimize customer engagement strategies.
  • Technical Consultants looking to integrate predictive analytics into their systems.
  • Data Analysts hoping to leverage AI capabilities for better forecasting.
  • Administrators and Developers keen on enhancing data platform functionalities.

Enhance Your Learning

To make the most of this live session, it would be beneficial to explore these eLearning courses:

  • Explore SAP Customer Data Platform Essentials
  • Administrating Customer Data Platform
  • Developing with SAP Customer Data Platform
  • Exploring the SAP Customer Data Platform's AI Workbench 

Additionally, check out a detailed Technical Blog that guides you on generating sample data to trigger default configurations for SAP Customer Data Platform AI Workbench Churn and Customer Lifetime Value (CLV) models. This blog contains invaluable insights to prepare for the session.

Register Now

Don’t miss this opportunity to leverage AI's predictive prowess in shaping your customer interactions. Secure your spot now at Predicting Customer Behavior with SAP Customer Data Platform AI Workbench Registration.

Event Details

  • Date: March 18, 2026
  • Time: 15:00 - 16:30 GMT+2
  • Virtual Platform: SAP Learning Site

Join us to explore the future of customer behaviour prediction and unlock new strategies for customer engagement success!

Stay Informed

Stay updated with the latest insights by subscribing to this blog post. Equip yourself with cutting-edge tools to predict and influence customer behaviour efficiently. We look forward to engaging with you and providing valuable updates directly through this platform.

entry.title: Setup and Activation Guide: Joule in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud
entry.content:
Prerequisites

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2021 or higher (2023 FPS01 recommended for full capabilities)
  • SAP BTP Global Account with Joule entitlements
  • SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition entitlements
  • Cloud Identity Services (IAS/IPS) setup
  • RISE with SAP contract (Joule is only available within RISE with SAP)
  • SAP-managed data center setup

Pre-Setup Checklist

  • Verify SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition version compatibility (minimum 2021, recommended 2023 FPS01+) 
  • Confirm system is registered in SAP BTP System Landscape
  • Ensure Cloud Connector connectivity is established
  • Validate minimum UI5 version requirements per your S/4HANA release 
  • Verify Joule and SAP Build Work Zone entitlements in BTP Global Account 
  • Confirm Identity Authentication Service (IAS) integration with S/4HANA system

Setup Steps

1. BTP Platform Configuration
  1. Create a dedicated BTP subaccount in a Joule-supported data center 
  2. Enable Cloud Foundry environment and create organization/space ,
  3. Add Joule (foundation plan) and SAP Build Work Zone entitlements to the subaccount 
  4. Add Cloud Identity Services (connectivity plan) entitlements ,
2. Trust and Security Setup
  1. Configure Trust to the Identity Authentication tenant in BTP subaccount 
  2. Configure trusted domains in SAP BTP (add S/4HANA Fiori Launchpad URL)
  3. Configure trusted domains in Identity Authentication Service 
  4. Disable default identity provider in BTP subaccount to avoid authentication conflicts
3. Content Provider Configuration
  1. Expose SAP Fiori Launchpad content to SAP BTP 
  2. Set up SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition as a content provider
  3. Create SAP Build Work Zone application and service instance 
  4. Configure destinations for system connectivity
4. Joule Service Activation
  1. Run the Joule Booster from BTP Global Account 
  2. Select the prepared subaccount and configure integration settings
  3. Create Joule formation and select required Cloud Identity Services
  4. Configure Identity Provisioning Service for user and role synchronization 
5. Fiori Launchpad Integration
  1. Access transaction /UI2/FLP in SAP S/4HANA system
  2. Navigate to Launchpad App Manager (Cross Client)
  3. Configure and activate Joule plug-in in target mapping 
  4. Set up Joule web client URL with proper application component (CA-FLP-EXT-JOU)

Post-Activation Validation

  • Verify Joule icon appears in Fiori Launchpad (diamond-shaped icon)
  • Test user authentication through configured Identity Authentication Service 
  • Validate navigation capabilities to core SAP Fiori apps (Procurement, Finance, Sales, Service Management) 
  • Confirm transactional capabilities like "show purchase orders" and "upload supplier invoice"
  • Check system performance and response times

Troubleshooting Common Issues

Issue Possible Cause Resolution
Joule icon not visiblePlugin not properly configured in target mappingVerify target mapping configuration and URL setup 
Authentication popup appearsTrust configuration mismatch between BTP and IASCheck trust configuration and trusted domains setup 
Navigation failuresMissing business roles or authorizationsEnsure users have appropriate SAP Fiori app roles assigned 
Formation not workingSystem not added to Joule formationAdd S/4HANA system to Joule formation or run booster again 

Best Practices

  • Ensure consistent Identity Authentication Service across all integrated SAP systems 
  • Use technical users with comprehensive authorizations for system connectivity 
  • Regularly update to latest S/4HANA releases for enhanced Joule capabilities 
  • Implement proper role-based access control to maintain security
  • Monitor system performance after Joule activation 

Next Steps

  • Navigate to Fiori Launchpad to access Joule web client and explore features ,
  • Train users on conversational patterns and available capabilities 
  • Consider extending Joule capabilities through SAP Build tools for custom scenarios
  • Plan for ongoing maintenance and system updates

entry.title: CFOs seeking AI – Optimal decisions using SAP IBP in Product Making (manufacturing)
entry.content:
INTRODUCTION - THE PROBLEM

Today, every business is seeking efficiency and productivity improvement with Artificial Intelligence. This means that drawing upon available data in systems, faster decisions can be made with Artificial Intelligence. However, most teams in FIN (Finance) are used to doing their analysis in spreadsheets offline, which works when decisions come to Finance for approval. However, Artificial Intelligence provides a shortcut - more decisions can be made away from the centre - decentralised - drawing on the available data sets in current systems. 

This article takes my earlier Article on  How Finance & Supply Chain can get closer together to a  new level, and brings more into focus my last Article on “Mind the Gap” – Improves ROI, Cost & Margin by Merging Planning Processes. 

UNDERSTANDING THE CONTEXT

Operations in Product Making (manufacturing) is perhaps the most decentralised location where decisions are being made at lightening speed - decisions that impact every area of the business: revenue, customer service, profit and working capital. However, these decisions are more often made on the basis of operational KPIs and not what is best for the business financially, because Operations don't have access to the financial implications of decisions until after the decisoin is made.

CFOs and FIN (Finance) Teams remotely setting Targets, KPIs and Performance Management is no longer enough when using Artificial Intelligence - its too slow! 

Operations in Product Making (manufacturing) have historically looked to Finance to validate financial outcomes of operational decisions. However, the advances taking place in Artificial Intelligence  mean that Operations can leverage Artificial Intelligence for rapid decision-making support, leaving Finance completely out of the short-term decision-making loop - unless - Finance provide the available FIN (Finance) data, currently held in spreadsheets, to the operational planning systems so that the  FIN (Finance) data can be used by Artificial Intelligence - especially the forward looking FIN (Finance) forecast data that is needed to guide forward Operational decision-making with Artificial Intelligence and to deliver the optimal financial decisions in Operations. 

SOLVING THE EMERGENT PROBLEM 

There are many ways financial data can be embedded in the system for use by Artificial Intelligence. My focus in this article is to talk to the rationale of using SAP Integrated Business Planning for Product Making (manufacturing) businesses.   

Why ?

SAP Integrated Business Planning is a collaborative decision-making platform that brings together in one space all the available past, present and future information required for decision making in Operations for Product Making (manufacturing) businesses. Operations use the available past, present and future information in systems to make operational decisions and trade-offs, today, that are felt by the business financially for the months that follow.  Artificial Intelligence provides a more efficient basis for making these decisions faster, based on the data available. 

What needs to change ?

FIN (Finance)data in systems is typically retrospective, or at best set up for transactional use in the present. Operations are making decisions today that impact the future, and forward looking FIN (Finance) data is most often held off-line in spreadsheets, enabling financial analysts to constantly iterate and update their analysis independently of the core business systems. This means Operations are making a myriad of daily decisions using FIN (Finance) information that is not the latest available. Depending on how out-of-date the FIN (Finance) data is in the business system, future decision-making will be financially sub-optimal and Artificial Intelligence will accelerate sub-optimal financial decision-making in Operations, today, with the impact of sub-optimal financial performance of the business as a whole, in the months that follow.  

Outside of SAP Integrated Business Planning, there are few options in the market that can boast native integration with underlying ERP systems - standard integration connections to both SAP ECC and S/4HANA - to bring past, present and future data together in one data space, and the ability to use embedded Artificial Intelligence without the complexity and cost of AI Agents, or Agentic AI - where integrations with Agentic AI need to be thoroughly tested prior to use.

THE SOLUTION FORWARD

Operations can make ALL decisions in SAP Integrated Business Planning. 

The accelerated adoption of Artificial Intelligence means faster decisions using the data transferred between ERP and Operations Planning. Using SAP Integrated Business Planning enables all the FIN (Finance) data that Finance want considered in the future using the rapid decision making of Artificial Intelligence can be made available to Operations and the optimal FIN (Finance) decision can be made - fast!

TODAY'S NEW CHALLENGES 

In short, Artificial Intelligence accelerates the need :

  • to implement SAP Integrated Business Planning in Product Making (manufacturing) businesses.
  • for FIN (Finance) to share FIN (Finance) Data across the business to assure the optimal business financial outcome.
  • for FIN (Finance) to lead implementation of new technologies across the business, prior to Artificial Intelligence adoption, to assure the optimal business financial outcome

entry.title: Missing Number Ranges in PRODUCTION SYSTEM
entry.content:
• The number range customizations for Customs Adaptation for Billing (Sub-Objects) maintained in DEV/TEST not available in PROD, even though the transport was executed successfully.
  • Manual maintenance of number ranges not possible in PROD in CBC activity 'Define Number Ranges for Billing Documents ' (missing “Add Row / Insert Line” in the SSCUI), and what is the supported way to create or correct these number ranges in the productive system.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Missing Number Ranges in PRODUCTION SYSTEM
entry.content:
For the number ranges which are missing in PROD, they need to be manually transport them into the PROD system.

  1. Choose the specific number range that needs to be transported.  
  2. Navigate to the menu ->interval-> transport.
  3. Choose the appropriate transport request. 
  4. Then transport.

The number range can be saved without put in customizing request. 

In CBC activity 'Define Number Ranges for Billing Documents ' of P system, you are not able to add new Number Range as the Add/Insert button is missing because this is a Standard system behavior. You need to create number ranges in the Q/T System and transport them to PROD system.

  • KBA 3692876  -Transport Behavior of Number Range Number, Interval, and Status in SAP S/4HANA Cloud in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me
  • KBA 3213369 -Number range for Billing and Sales is configured in Q and transported to P - S/4HANA Cloud - SAP for Me

entry.title: Simplified Freight Order in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Transportation Management
entry.content:
Simplified Freight Order in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Transportation Management

Target Audience

This series is for customers using SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition who want to adopt a simplified, lightweight transportation management process. It’s also relevant for partners and consultants working on SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition implementation projects.

 

1. Overview 

If your logistics are lightweight, full-scale Transportation Management can feel heavy. With SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Transportation Management, you can automate most steps so planning, execution, and settlement take only a few clicks. 

This series highlights practical automations across the freight order lifecycle. In the overview illustration, steps marked in blue can be fully automated.

Picture 1: Automation possibilities in freight order management. 
* The subcontracting ‘Send to carrier’ API is planned for a future release; timelines may change. Check the API Hub for current availability. 

You can tailor automation to your process needs. Examples in this series are starting points you can adapt to your individual business. 

Important Links: 

  • Product documentation: Transportation Management in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud 
  • Business Event Consumption Implementation Guide 
  • SAP Business Accelerator Hub – Freight Order API Overview 

 

2. Simplified Freight Order Planning 

This chapter focuses on simplifying and automating freight unit creation and freight order planning.

 

2.1 Delivery-Based Freight Units

You can configure the system to automatically create freight units when deliveries are created. Once sales orders and deliveries exist, planning can start immediately. 

Configuration blog post: https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blog-posts-by-sap/delivery-integration-in-sap-s-4hana-public-cloud-transportation-management/ba-p/14306554 

 2.2 Focused Transportation Cockpit

You can simplify the Transportation Cockpit layout to focus on the data and actions most relevant to daily planning. 

Configuration blog post: https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blog-posts-by-sap/simplified-transportation-cockpit-in-sap-s-4hana-public-cloud/ba-p/14306615 

 
2.3 Automatic Assignment of Capacity Requirements 

  • By using requirement grouping profiles, you can automatically assign freight units to freight orders based on criteria such as source location or carrier, reducing the manual planning effort. 
  • Configuration details:  https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/61e246f4b34c4e1790d8b7651c0b40a8/5a4fe7bd5ba04412a19f016d4346e48f.html?locale=en-US 

2.4 Automated Freight Order Planning 

You can automate freight order creation end to end by consuming local business events. Section 3.1 explains the basics of local business event consumption that you can adjust for planning automation.  

You need to implement logic to group freight units into freight orders while respecting vehicle capacities and other constraints. 

Details on API actions, like retrieving relevant data, create freight order or assign freight units, can be found in the API hub: 
https://api.sap.com/api/CE_FREIGHTORDER_0001/resource/Freight_Order  

 

3. Automate Freight Order Updates 

Several freight order attributes and statuses must be set before transportation execution. You can automate these updates, for example via Business Events and APIs, to eliminate manual steps. 

Details are provided in the following sections. 

3.1 Freight Order Header Data 

If you use simple rules for assigning data like purchasing organisation, purchasing group, and carrier at freight order header level, you can fully automate this via local Business Event consumption and the Freight Order API. 

  • Implementation blog post: https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blog-posts-by-sap/event-driven-tm-in-s-4hana-public-cloud-update-delivery-data-when-a-freight/ba-p/14306637 
  • Good to know: This example can serve as a template for other event-based and API-based automations. 

3.2 Freight Order Subcontracting 

  • An API for the Send to carrier action is planned for a future release; timelines may change. Check the SAP Business Accelerator Hub (API Hub) for availability. 
  • If subcontracting isn’t required in your process, you can omit this step without additional configuration. 

3.3 Ready for Transportation Execution 

  • After planning and before warehouse tasks begin, the freight order must be set to  
    ready for transportation execution.  
  • You can automate this via local Business Events as described in chapter 3.1 by subscribing, for example, to the event changed of the freight order 
  • It is essential to define clear conditions that determine the appropriate time to update the ready for transportation execution status. 

 4. Physical Transportation Execution 


4.1 Picking & Packing 

Picking and packing are typically manual steps. If needed, you can automate parts of these activities using Warehouse Management APIs. 

More information: 

  • IM based warehouses: 
    https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/51e9c353b677b44ce10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US&state=DRAFT&version=2602.500&q=inventory+management  
  • Cloud ERP‑based warehouses:  
    https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/87f9b54f9c4f4e75aff0061860a6589a/4938549c790d4e2cbeade042b59becd3.html?locale=en-US&version=2508.500  


4.5 Loading 

Transportation Management in S/4HANA Public Cloud supports implicit loading. When a freight order stage is set to check-out, the system automatically marks all items and corresponding stops as loaded. No configuration is needed; this is standard behavior. 

The following articles provide further information: 

  • https://me.sap.com/notes/0003671723 
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/61e246f4b34c4e1790d8b7651c0b40a8/d7535b4edc504d38860d38beedc11d2b.html?locale=en-US&version=2602.500 

 

4.6 Check-Out at Freight Order Stop 

Check out of a freight order is a key lifecycle step. It triggers the following actions: 

  • Implicit loading of all assigned items 
  • Creation of freight documents for printing  
  • Creation and sending of ASN documents 
  • Handover to settlement by setting the freight order posting status to ready for confirmation 

Best practice: Trigger check-out after physical loading is complete.  You can use the goods issue event on the delivery as the trigger to post the check-out event.  
The system allows you to post goods issue before or after check-out. 

A reference of relevant events can be found here: https://api.sap.com/event/CE_OUTBOUNDDELIVERYEVENTS/overview 

4.7 Print Freight Documents 

You can configure printing to run fully automatically at freight order check-out. 

Documentation: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/61e246f4b34c4e1790d8b7651c0b40a8/f8b089eaf4374e57976abafc9c1252a2.html  


5. Freight Settlement

Freight Order also enables precise calculation of freight costs, which can be performed either during the planning phase or after the physical shipment is executed. To achieve accurate cost determination, Freight Agreements and Rate Tables must be maintained in the system.

  • Freight Agreements define the contractual terms between the shipper and the carrier, including agreed pricing conditions, surcharges, and validity periods.
  • Rate Tables store the structured rate information—such as charges based on distance, weight, volume, or other relevant parameters—which the system uses to determine the applicable freight cost.

Freight settlement can be initiated only after the following prerequisites are fulfilled. First, the freight cost must be successfully calculated in the corresponding freight order. Second, the checkout event must be triggered to indicate completion of the transportation execution.

Once these conditions are met, the posting status in the freight order charges tab changes to “Ready for Confirmation,” signifying that the freight order is eligible for accrual posting. Accrual posting can be performed either manually using the “Monitor Accrual Postings” app or automatically through the condition management framework.

After the freight cost is confirmed and posted, the system creates the corresponding service purchase order and service entry sheet (SES). Subsequently, the carrier invoice can be posted against the SES. Finally, the freight costs are transferred to Finance and posted either as an expense or to the material ledger, depending on the applicable business process.

Overall, this end-to-end process not only ensures accurate freight cost calculation but also strengthens compliance and operational efficiency by linking transportation execution with financial settlement in a unified system. 

Documentation: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/61e246f4b34c4e1790d8b7651c0b40a8/9eb4cd8d3f5146c286c978dfbc0ac0fb.html?version=2602.500

6. Further Information

A comprehensive blog post about the Transportation Management process and the relevant scope items can be found here:
https://community.sap.com/t5/delivery-transportation/transportation-management/ta-p/226349

entry.title: New POD 2.0 Framework: The New Generation of SAP Digital Manufacturing Worker UIs
entry.content:
This blog post is part of a series on SAP Digital Manufacturing new POD 2.0 framework, the new generation of SAP Digital Manufacturing worker UIs.

The next post on the series will be published as soon as this one reaches 20 Likes, so please leave a Like! 👍

🗓️ Timeline

POD 2.0 is currently in BETA with selected customers, General Availability (GA) is planned for 2605 release.

📢Introduction

SAP Digital Manufacturing is taking a major step forward with POD 2.0. This is not a cosmetic upgrade. It is a full redesign of how worker-facing applications are built, configured, and executed on the shop floor.

If you work with Production Operator Dashboards (PODs) today, you already know their importance. They are the primary interface between operators and manufacturing execution. POD 2.0 fundamentally changes how these UIs are built, behave, how fast they respond, and how easily they can be adapted to real-world processes.

❓ What Is POD 2.0?

POD 2.0 is a new UI framework inside SAP Digital Manufacturing for building worker-facing execution screens.

It replaces the architectural foundation of the existing POD model with a faster, more modular, and highly configurable framework.

The goal is simple:

Give operators faster, cleaner, purpose-driven screens, while giving consultants and customers far more flexibility with minimal coding.

Instead of rigid POD types and heavy customization, POD 2.0 introduces a modern configuration-driven model that lets you shape execution UIs around real production needs.

This is not a side feature. It is the future direction for all new worker UI capabilities in SAP Digital Manufacturing. 🚀

❓ Why POD 2.0?

The original POD framework served many scenarios, but real customer projects exposed recurring challenges:

  • Screen load delays during execution

  • Overly complex UI structures

  • Limited layout flexibility

  • Heavy reliance on custom plugins

  • Disconnects between discrete and process scenarios

  • Hard-to-maintain custom logic

On the shop floor, even small inefficiencies matter. Waiting seconds for screens to refresh adds up across shifts, lines, and operators.

POD 2.0 was designed to solve these problems at the framework level, not with incremental patches. 🚀

🔍What Makes POD 2.0 Different?

1. Performance First

POD 2.0 dramatically reduces load and refresh times.

Operators interact with responsive screens that feel immediate. Faster UI cycles directly translate to:

  • Reduced operator idle time

  • Smoother takt flow

  • Less friction during high-frequency operations

This is not just a technical improvement. It impacts real production throughput. ⏱️

2. Template-Based Design Instead of POD Types

Traditional POD types are gone.

Instead of choosing rigid categories like Work Center or Order PODs, POD 2.0 introduces POD templates. These act as smart starting points that can be reshaped freely.

This removes historical trade-offs like:

“Do I pick this POD type for feature A and lose feature B?”

Now you design around the process, not framework constraints. 🛠️

3. Visual WYSIWYG Designer

POD 2.0 includes a true WYSIWYG (“What You See Is What You Get”) visual designer:

  • Drag-and-drop layout control

  • Inline formatting

  • Dynamic labels and fields

  • Dialog creation

  • Buttons and actions

and much more!

❗❗What previously required UI extensions or custom plugins can now be configured directly.❗❗

This enables:

  • Faster project delivery

  • Reduced development dependency

  • Easier iteration with customers

Even non-developer teams can adjust execution screens quickly. 🧑‍💻

4. Modular Plugin Architecture

Large monolithic plugins have been broken into smaller, focused components.

Benefits include:

  • Better performance

  • Cleaner configuration

  • Reduced customization overhead

  • Easier extension handling

You compose functionality instead of wrestling with oversized plugins. 🧩

5. Rich Context Access

POD 2.0 exposes contextual execution data directly to the UI layer.

Instead of repeatedly calling backend services through complex production processes, many values are already available in context. This allows:

  • Leaner production processes

  • Faster interactions

  • Cleaner logic flows

It is a performance and maintainability win. 🚀

6. Advanced UI Interaction Capabilities

You can now configure:

  • Dynamic dialogs

  • Conditional actions

  • Context-driven buttons

  • External URL integrations

  • Embedded workflows

Example scenarios include:

  • Guided operator confirmations

  • Digital batch record steps

  • Inline data capture dialogs

  • Automated process triggers

These interactions previously required custom development. Now they are configuration-driven. 🖥️

and much more, which I will cover in detail in the upcoming blog posts of this series.

❓ What This Means for Customers

POD 2.0 represents a strategic shift:

  • New execution features will target the new framework

  • Legacy pod architecture will gradually phase out

  • Performance and usability expectations are reset

For new implementations, POD 2.0 should be evaluated as the primary execution UI strategy.

For existing landscapes, migration planning becomes an important architectural discussion.

The next post on the series will be published as soon as this one reaches 20 Likes, so please leave a Like! 👍

Stay tuned for more! Thanks,
Manoel Costa

entry.title: Migration of Liquidity Planner Data (FLQITEMFI → FQM_FLOW) to S/4HANA via FQM_TRANSFER
entry.content:
Hello,

we would like to migrate our cash data from the Liquidity Planner in the ECC system to our new SAP S/4HANA system. In SAP In SAP Note 3626786 – S/4HANA Cash Management: Transaction FQM_TRANSFER FAQ, the use of a web service based on FQM_WS_DISTRIBUTE and the consumer proxy CO_FQM_WS_DISTRIBUTE is mentioned. However, there is no detailed description or example provided on how this configuration should look in practice.

However, it is not clear to us how the actual data conversion from the legacy table FLQITEMFI to the new S/4HANA table FQM_FLOW is performed.

Additionally, our S/4HANA system is a sandbox system, and during the migration phase the original ECC system will no longer be available. Therefore, we do not understand how the web service–based transfer can be configured or executed in this scenario. We also could not find detailed documentation describing this process step by step.

Could you please clarify:

  • How the conversion from FLQITEMFI to FQM_FLOW is technically handled?

  • Whether the web service is mandatory for this conversion, especially if the ECC system is no longer available?

  • If there are any alternative methods or tools to migrate or convert the liquidity planning data directly into FQM_FLOW in S/4HANA?

Thank you very much for your support.

entry.title: Unlocking the Power of Story Reports in SAP SuccessFactors: A Deep Dive into Onboarding Metrics
entry.content:
I recently came across an ask from client where they wanted us to show the important onboarding metrics that would help them to process their new hires onboarding much faster and conveniently. By including these metrics, the HR can easily identify the gaps, and bridge them to initiate their onboarding process or to easily prepare for Day 1 activities for new hires.

Requirement-

The ask was to report on some key metrics that are highly used in Onboarding namely- 

  • Manage Pending Hires
  • Days to Initiate Onboarding

What I did-

There are no such direct fields to capture these fields which made it to the customized story report for Onboarding. I used calculated column and creating some logical conditions to report on these metrics. Let's see how we can achieve the output for these metrics.

  1. Manage Pending Hires:
    • Completed
    • Not Completed
  2. Days to Initiate Onboarding:
    • 1 day or less 
    • 2–3 days
    • More than 3 days

 

  1. Manage Pending Hires-

In SAP SuccessFactors, the Manage Pending Hires tool streamlines the transition of new hires by connecting data from Recruitment Management (RCM) and Onboarding (ONB) to create active Employee Central (EC) records.

 

  • Manage Pending Hires- Completed

Objective:
This metric aims at tracking how many new hires have completed the "Manage Pending Hires" (MPH) step. This is critical for monitoring onboarding readiness.

Calculated Column Logic:

IF (Hire Status="Hired" AND (Process Status="Completed" OR Process Status=Open"), "Yes", "No”)

Expected Output:

 

  • Manage Pending Hires- Not Completed
    Objective:
    This metric track and oversee new hires who have completed recruitment but are not yet fully integrated into Employee Central (EC).

Calculated Column Logic:

IF (Hire Status="Ready to Hire" AND Process Status="Open"), "Yes", "No")

Expected Output:

 

This gives stakeholders a quick view of onboarding progress across a hiring period.

  1. Days to Initiate Onboarding:

"Days to Initiate Onboarding" typically refers to the number of days it takes for a new employee to begin the onboarding process after they have accepted an offer. This metric can help organizations assess the efficiency of their onboarding procedures and identify any delays in getting new hires or clients started with the necessary training, resources, and integration into the company or system.

 

  • Days to Initiate Onboarding (DTIO) One day or less.

"Days to Initiate Onboarding 1 or less" suggests that the goal is to initiate the onboarding process within one day or less after the new employee or client has accepted the offer or signed the contract. This indicates a very efficient onboarding process, aiming to minimize delays and ensure that new hires or clients can start their integration as quickly as possible.

Calculated Column Logic:

DTIO (Days to Initiate Onboarding) = DAYS BETWEEN (Application Date, Initiate Onboarding Step Complete Date)

DTIO 1 or Less= IF([DTIO]<=1, "Yes”, “No”)

Expected Output:

  • Days to initiate Onboarding (DTIO) 2–3 days.

"Days to Initiate ONB 2-3 Days" means that the goal is to start the onboarding process within a time frame of two to three days after a new employee or client has accepted an offer or signed a contract. This indicates a moderate approach to onboarding, allowing for a bit more time compared to the "1 or less" or "2 or less" goals, while still aiming to initiate the process quickly. It suggests that the organization may have specific steps or preparations that require this additional time before onboarding can officially begin.

 

Calculated Column Logic:

DTIO 2-3 Days= IF([DTIO]=2 OR ([DTIO]=3),"Yes”, “No")

Expected Output:

  • Days to Initiate Onboarding (DTIO) more than 3 days.

Days to Initiate ONB More than 3 Days" indicates that the onboarding process is expected to start more than three days after a new employee or client has accepted an offer or signed a contract. This timeframe suggests a less urgent approach to onboarding, which may be due to various factors such as organizational processes, resource availability, or the complexity of the onboarding requirements. It may also imply that there are specific preparations or steps that need to be completed before the onboarding can officially begin. This could potentially lead to delays in the integration of new hires or clients into the organization.

 

Calculated Column Logic:

DTIO Over 3 Days= IF([DTIO]>3,"Yes","No")

Expected Output:

Lesson Learnt-

Adding these metrics really helped the HRs to give a large point of view where they stand in terms of onboarding process and also shows some alarming situations specially for new hires for which Days to initiate onboarding is more than 3 days.

 

Since these values are not directly reportable on story reports, using calculated column feature and logical condition "IF" might help you to report on such metrics.

entry.title: Migration Cockpit in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
Welcome to your one-stop shop to learn and share knowledge about SAP's ready-to-run public cloud ERP software solution.  

SAP Cloud ERP is part of SAP Business Suite that brings AI, data, and applications together into a fully integrated system. This community is open for anyone in the cloud ERP ecosystem–customers, partners, and SAP experts.  

Migration Cockpit

  • Explore Migration Cockpit
  • Using Staging Tables as Intermediate Storage
  • Transfer List
  • Increased File Size Limits for an Efficient Data Load

Explore Migration Objects: A New SAP Fiori App for Enhanced Project Planning

A successful data migration relies on meticulous planning and a deep understanding of data dependencies. With the 2602 release, we are introducing the SAP Fiori app, "Explore Migration Objects," to bring a new level of clarity to your project preparation.

This app provides crucial insights into migration object dependencies and their relationships to specific scope items. It empowers your project team to efficiently plan data migration projects by ensuring that all mandatory and optional predecessor objects are correctly identified and migrated in the proper sequence. Through interactive graphs, you can now visualize how migration objects are interconnected. The app allows for detailed dependency analysis, differentiating between mandatory and optional predecessors, and provides focused search and direct access to migration object documentation. By visualizing these relationships, your team can make informed decisions, ultimately reducing project risks and optimizing timelines. Please note that this initial version of the app currently supports migration objects built for the "Migrate Data Using Staging Tables" approach.

 

 

Enhanced Flexibility with Staging Tables for Direct System Migration

For customers using the "Migrate Data Directly from SAP System" approach, the 2602 release introduces a powerful new capability: the use of staging tables as intermediate storage. This feature is designed to facilitate the adjustment of selected data before it is loaded into SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

This enhancement significantly increases transparency and provides robust validation capabilities for your selected data. It gives your team more flexibility to adjust and cleanse application data from the source SAP system before the Now, data adaptation is performed within ETL tool prior to loading the data into the migration cockpit’s staging tables. Once the data is prepared and validated, you can transfer the ready-for-processing data to the SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit for further processing, ensuring higher data quality in your new cloud environment.

 

 

 

Streamline Your Migration Troubleshooting with the New Transfer List

Transfer List

In any complex data migration project, ensuring data is transferred accurately is paramount. When issues arise during the simulation or migration phases, pinpointing the root cause can be time-consuming. To bring greater transparency to this process, we are introducing the Transfer List for the migration approach "Migrate Data Directly from SAP System." This feature is designed for data migration specialists and project teams who need deep visibility into the data transfer process for effective and efficient troubleshooting.


A Clear View for Faster Resolution

The Transfer List provides a detailed view of the data exactly as it is passed to the target API that creates the records in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.
This capability is particularly beneficial when you need to troubleshoot the simulation or migration process. For example, your team can now analyze the Transfer List to definitively determine whether an issue lies with the target API itself or if it is related to a problem in the structure or field mapping. This clarity helps you isolate problems faster, reduce resolution time, and keep your migration project on track.

To use this feature, the simulation or migration process must first be completed for the relevant migration object instance. Once complete, you can access the Transfer List with the following steps:

1. From the Migration Project screen, select the "Instances" link for your object.
2. On the Migration Object Instances screen, select the instance you wish to investigate.
3. Choose the "Transfer List" button.

On the subsequent screen, you can view the target parameters, fields, and their corresponding values that were used in the transfer, providing your team with the precise information needed for analysis. This enhancement empowers you to resolve issues with confidence and precision, ensuring a smoother transition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

Increased File Size Limits for an Efficient Data Load

To further streamline your data migration activities, we have increased the file size limits for the "Migrate Data Using Staging Tables" approach. This practical update allows for more efficient handling of large data volumes.

When you upload your data using XML files, you can bundle multiple files into a single zip file for a more convenient upload. With this release, the maximum size for each individual XML file contained within the zip file has been increased to 400MB. This allows you to process larger data sets in a single file. Please be aware that while individual files can be larger, the total size of the zip file itself should not exceed 100MB upon upload to the migration cockpit. This improvement simplifies the data loading process and reduces the number of uploads required for extensive data sets.

I hope you're as excited about these updates as I am! To learn more, I encourage you to explore the SAP Help Portal and join the conversation on the SAP Community.

What is New?

Check Migration Cockpit Community

SAP Help Data Migration

SAP Community YouTube presented by Claudia Gemm and Elizaveta Kozhevenikova

  

📽️ Watch the replays of our SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Early Release Series! 

From January 12-16, we hosted a series of 22 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find the recording and presentation for Migration Cockpit as well as 21 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime. 

🔎 Find all our assets 

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: UI5 MessageStrip - Assertion failed: Method 'class' must be called with exactly one class name
entry.content:
Hi, 

I discovered this in a UI5 webapp (not using Fiori Elements) while developing. 

Component: sap.m.MessageStrip
Version affected: 1.143.0+ (until current, 1.144.1)
Version working: 1.142.6
Error: Assertion failed: Method 'class' must be called with exactly one class name
Reproduction: <MessageStrip class="sapUiSmallMargin" text="Test"/>

Even removing the class attribute triggers this failure. There is no MessageStrip in the code, that has more than one CSS class element. There is furthermore no usage of addStyleClass() etc. in the controller logic.

entry.title: Availability Control Issues in budget profile with actitity groups ++
entry.content:
We have created budget profiles with activity group ++ with Action 3 and usage 100% for our wbs projects. These budget profiles have only annual values activated in order to update and maintain budget year by year.

The intention was to trigger availability control if a PR/PO/SES/MIGO/MIRO exceed the approved budget during the year.


What we are facing is that SAP allows to create SES/MIGO  with  cases from previous years (commitments)  for WBS elements that do not have any approved budget for the current year, and post actuals.

We do not use carry forward transactions (budget//commitments) as a business policy. 

Is there any case that the ++ group transaction is not sufficient for our case and we should register additionally in the budget profile another transaction entry?

entry.title: When to Use SAP Central Invoice Management (CIM) vs. SAP S/4HANA Convergent Invoicing
entry.content:
Introduction

Organizations often struggle with managing invoices efficiently—whether it’s supplier invoices in procurement or customer invoices in complex billing scenarios. SAP offers two powerful solutions: Central Invoice Management (CIM) and Convergent Invoicing (CI) in S/4HANA. While both deal with invoices, they serve very different business needs.

This blog explores real-world business stories to help you decide when to use each.

 

Business Story 1: Global Procurement and Vendor Invoice Chaos

Scenario:
A multinational company receives thousands of supplier invoices daily in multiple formats—PDFs, scanned documents, and even paper. Manual entry leads to delays, compliance risks, and missed early payment discounts.

Solution:
SAP Central Invoice Management (CIM) is the hero here.

Key Features of CIM:

  • Central capture of invoices via OCR and document extraction
  • Intelligent AI/ML-driven classification and matching to purchase orders
  • Fiori-based apps for end-to-end tracking, status monitoring, and approvals
  • Role-based access control and audit-ready workflows
  • Built on SAP BTP for extensibility and seamless cloud integration

End to end process flow

  1. Invoice Capture
  • Supplier sends invoice via email, portal, or paper
  • CIM uses OCR and AI/ML to extract data and classify the invoice 

2.Invoice Validation & Matching

  • CIM matches invoice against Purchase Order (PO) and Goods Receipt.
  • Discrepancies flagged for approval workflow.

3.Approval Workflow

  • Fiori-based apps route invoice to approvers.
  • Role-based access ensures compliance and audit readiness.

4.Posting to S/4HANA

  • Approved invoice posted in S/4HANA as an AP document:
    • Debit: Expense or Inventory
    • Credit: Vendor Payables

5.Payment Execution

  • Standard Payment Run (F110) in S/4HANA:
    • Debit: Vendor Payables
    • Credit: Bank Clearing Account

6.Reporting & Monitoring

  • CIM dashboard tracks invoice status, approvals, and payment completion.

 

 Business Story 2: Telecom Billing Complexity

Scenario:

A telecom provider offers subscriptions, usage-based services, and partner settlements. Customers expect a single consolidated invoice, but billing data comes from multiple systems and services. So the goal is to consolidate multiple billing items into one customer invoice.

Solution:

SAP S/4HANA Convergent Invoicing (CI) steps in.

Key Features of CI:

  • Aggregates diverse billing events (usage, subscriptions, services) into one invoice
  • Uses FI‑CA (Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable) as the financial backbone
  • Offers rating, billing, and invoicing capabilities for high-volume, consumption-based services
  • Available via both classic SAP menu and modern Fiori applications

End to End process flow

1.Billing Data Collection

  • Usage records, subscription fees, and partner charges collected from multiple systems.

2.Rating & Charging

  • BRIM component calculates charges based on usage and pricing rules.

3.Billing Document Creation

  • CI aggregates all charges into a single billing document.

4.Invoice Generation

  • Invoice created in S/4HANA:
  • Debit: Customer Receivables
  • Credit: Revenue Accounts

5.Revenue Recognition

  • Revenue recognized based on IFRS/GAAP rules (e.g., at service delivery or billing).

6.Payment Collection

  • Customer pays via standard AR processes:
  • Debit: Bank Account
  • Credit: Customer Receivables

7.Reporting & Analytics

  • Fiori apps provide billing and revenue insights.
Summary
Key Differences at a Glance
AspectCentral Invoice managementConvergent Invoicing
Focus
Supplier/Vendor invoices (AP)
Customer invoices (AR)
Domain
Procure-to-Pay
Order-to-Cash
Technology
SAP BTP, OCR, AI
S/4HANA BRIM, FI-CA
Use case
Invoice capture & approval
 Billing consolidation
